description: Implement learn:execute command with phase management, personalization, and CLI State API integration
approach:
**Implementation Strategy**:

This solution implements the learn:execute command following a systematic 7-phase approach:

1. **Schema Extensions First**: Extend learn-plan.schema.json and learn-state.schema.json to support phase management with backward compatibility
2. **CLI API Foundation**: Implement learn:read-session and learn:update-progress commands in the CLI layer
3. **Phase Algorithm**: Implement assignPhases() using topological sort + BFS for automatic phase assignment
4. **Command Implementation**: Create learn:execute command with 5-stage execution flow (init → deliver → verify → update → feedback)
5. **Personalization Layer**: Integrate profile data to customize resource presentation, learning order, time suggestions, and difficulty
6. **Phase Transition**: Implement phase advancement logic with validation
7. **Testing & Documentation**: Comprehensive test coverage and documentation updates

**Key Design Principles**:
- Strict CLI State API usage (no direct file operations)
- Simulated Agent pattern (AskUserQuestion orchestration) for interactive experience
- Phase-constrained execution (same phase flexible, cross-phase blocked)
- User-choice verification (mcp-runner vs self-report for practical_task)
- Full profile-driven personalization (style, approach, time_availability, experience_level, known_topics)
- Atomic operations with withLearnLock() for concurrency safety

**Backward Compatibility**:
- Auto-detect and backfill missing phase information in existing plans
- Graceful handling of legacy data structures

tasks.title: Extend learn-plan.schema.json for phase management
tasks.description: Add phase field to knowledge_points and phases array at root level to support phase-based learning progression. Include backward compatibility for existing plans.

modification_points.change: Add phases array with phase_number, phase_name, knowledge_point_ids, description, status

implementation: Read existing learn-plan.schema.json to understand current structure
Add phase field to knowledge_points.items.properties: { type: 'integer', minimum: 1, description: 'Phase number this knowledge point belongs to' }

Add phases array at root level with schema: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'object', properties: { phase_number, phase_name, knowledge_point_ids, description, status }, required: ['phase_number', 'phase_name', 'knowledge_point_ids'] } }
Add phase_name enum values: ['Foundation', 'Core Concepts', 'Advanced Topics', 'Specialization', 'Mastery']
Ensure backward compatibility by making phase field optional in knowledge_points
Document schema changes with examples

unit: Schema validates plans with phase information
Schema validates plans without phase information (backward compatibility)
Schema rejects invalid phase status values

criteria: Schema includes phase field in knowledge_points (integer, minimum: 1)
Schema includes phases array at root with all required fields
Schema validates both new (with phases) and legacy (without phases) plans
All existing test plans pass validation
verification: Validate existing plan.json files against updated schema
Verify backward compatibility with legacy plans

commit.message_template:
feat(learn): extend plan schema for phase management

Add phase field to knowledge points and phases array to root level.
Support phase-based learning progression with backward compatibility.

Add current_phase field: { type: ['integer', 'null'], minimum: 1, description: 'Current active phase number for the active session' }
Set default value to null (no active phase)
Document the field purpose and usage

unit: Schema validates state with current_phase as integer
Schema validates state with current_phase as null

criteria: Schema includes current_phase field (integer or null, minimum: 1)
Schema validation passes for both null and valid integer values
verification: Verify state.json validates with new field

tasks.title: Implement learn:read-session CLI command

implementation: Add learnReadSessionCommand function in learn.ts following existing patterns

Read .workflow/learn/sessions/{session-id}/plan.json
Load and compile learn-plan.schema.json for validation
Validate plan data against schema
Return { ok: true, data: plan } on success
Return { ok: false, error: {...} } on failure with proper error codes (NOT_FOUND, SCHEMA_INVALID, IO_ERROR)
Support --json flag for machine-readable output
Add command routing in cli.ts for 'learn:read-session'

unit: Returns plan data for valid session
Returns NOT_FOUND error for missing session
Returns SCHEMA_INVALID error for corrupted plan

criteria: Proper error handling for missing/invalid sessions

Read current plan.json
Find knowledge point by kp-id
Update status field (validate against enum: pending, in_progress, completed, skipped, optional)
Update _metadata.last_updated timestamp

tasks.title: Implement assignPhases algorithm in learn:plan
tasks.description: Implement phase assignment algorithm using topological sort + BFS to automatically assign phases based on DAG dependency levels. Integrate into learn:plan generation.

modification_points.change: Add assignPhases() function call after DAG generation

implementation: Add computeTopologicalLevels(knowledgePoints) function: Use BFS to compute dependency level for each KP, return Map<kp-id, level>
Add getPhaseNameByNumber(phaseNum) function: Map phase numbers to names ['Foundation', 'Core Concepts', 'Advanced Topics', 'Specialization', 'Mastery']

Add assignPhases(knowledgePoints, dependencyGraph) function: Call computeTopologicalLevels(), determine max level, calculate phase count (min 1, max 5), assign phase to each KP based on level distribution, create phases array with metadata
Integrate assignPhases() into plan generation flow after DAG construction
Add phase information to generated plan.json
Handle edge cases: single KP (1 phase), linear chain, complex DAG
Add backward compatibility check: if existing plan lacks phases, auto-assign on read

unit: Handles complex DAG with multiple roots
All KPs in same level get same phase

criteria: Phase count is dynamic (1-5 based on KP count and levels)
Each KP has phase field assigned
Root-level phases array is created with metadata
Backward compatibility for plans without phases
verification: Generate plans with different KP counts

commit.message_template:
feat(learn): implement automatic phase assignment

tasks.title: Implement Phase 1: Initialization in learn:execute
tasks.description: Implement initialization phase: read state, load session, determine target knowledge point, validate phase constraints.
implementation: Parse command arguments: extract KP-ID or --next-phase flag
Read global state: call ccw learn:read-state --json, extract active_session_id and current_phase
Validate active session exists
Read session plan: call ccw learn:read-session --session-id {id} --json
Determine target KP: if KP-ID provided, find in plan; else find next pending/in_progress KP
Validate phase constraint: if KP-ID provided and KP.phase != current_phase, show error and suggest --next-phase
Load profile: call ccw learn:read-profile --profile-id {id} --json
Initialize execution context: { session, plan, targetKP, profile, currentPhase }
Handle --next-phase flag: validate current phase complete, advance to next phase, update state
Display initialization summary: session goal, target KP, current phase

unit: Finds next pending KP when no ID provided

criteria: State and session are loaded via CLI commands
Error messages are clear and actionable
verification: Test with valid KP-ID in same phase
Test with KP-ID in different phase (should block)

commit.message_template:
feat(learn): implement execute Phase 1 initialization

Load state, session, profile and validate phase constraints.

tasks.title: Implement Phase 2: Content Delivery in learn:execute
tasks.description: Implement content delivery phase: display resources with personalization, provide learning activities, support note-taking.
implementation: Display KP title and description

Apply resource personalization based on profile.learning_preferences.style: practical → prioritize code examples and tutorials, theoretical → prioritize documentation and articles, visual → prioritize videos and diagrams
Sort resources by quality (gold > silver > bronze) and style preference
Display resources with quality indicators and summaries
Provide time estimate based on profile.time_availability.hours_per_week
Use AskUserQuestion to offer actions: 'Start learning', 'Take notes', 'Skip resource', 'Mark complete'
Implement note-taking: if user chooses 'Take notes', use AskUserQuestion to collect notes, save to sessions/{id}/interactions/notes/{kp-id}.md
Track learning time: record start time, calculate duration on completion
Display learning tips based on profile.learning_preferences.approach: theory-first → suggest reading docs first, practice-first → suggest trying code first, mixed → suggest alternating
Wait for user to complete learning before proceeding

unit: Resources are sorted by quality and style preference

criteria: Learning tips are relevant to profile
verification: Test with different learning styles (practical, theoretical, visual)
Test with different approaches (theory-first, practice-first, mixed)

commit.message_template:
feat(learn): implement execute Phase 2 content delivery

Personalized resource presentation with note-taking support.

tasks.title: Implement Phase 3: Assessment Verification in learn:execute
tasks.description: Implement assessment verification phase: execute assessments using mcp-runner or AskUserQuestion based on type and user preference.
implementation: Check assessment type from targetKP.assessment.type

For code_challenge: MUST use mcp-runner.js, display challenge description, wait for user to write solution, run mcp-runner with solution code, parse results (tests_passed, tests_total, score), determine pass/fail (score >= 0.7)
For practical_task: ask user preference (mcp-runner or self-report), if mcp-runner: same flow as code_challenge, if self-report: use AskUserQuestion to confirm completion and collect evidence
For multiple_choice: use AskUserQuestion to present options, validate answer, provide feedback
Handle assessment failures: allow up to 3 retries, provide hints after each failure, offer to skip after 3 failures
Record assessment results: { type, passed, score, attempts, timestamp, evidence }
Save results to sessions/{id}/interactions/jit-assessments.json
Display assessment feedback: pass/fail status, score, areas for improvement

criteria: Assessment results are recorded with evidence
verification: Test practical_task with both verification methods
Verify retry logic and skip option

commit.message_template:
feat(learn): implement execute Phase 3 assessment

Multi-type assessment with mcp-runner and user-choice verification.

tasks.title: Implement Phase 4: State and Profile Update in learn:execute
tasks.description: Implement state update phase: update KP status, update profile with new evidence, update proficiency and confidence scores.
implementation: Update KP status: call ccw learn:update-progress --session-id {id} --kp-id {kp} --status completed (or skipped if user skipped)

Update profile known_topics: read current profile, find or create topic entry for each targetKP.topic_refs, add new evidence object: { evidence_type: 'tool-verified' or 'self-report', kind: assessment.type, timestamp, summary, data: assessment results, verification_metadata: { method: 'mcp-runner', test_results, confidence_source } }
Calculate proficiency update: if tool-verified and score >= 0.7, increase proficiency by 0.1 (max 1.0), if self-report, increase by 0.05
Calculate confidence update: tool-verified → confidence = 0.8-1.0 based on score, self-report → confidence = 0.4-0.6
Update last_updated timestamp for each topic
Write updated profile: call ccw learn:write-profile --profile-id {id} --data '{json}'
Handle errors: if profile update fails, log error but don't block progression
Display update summary: topics updated, proficiency changes, evidence added

unit: Evidence is added with correct structure

criteria: KP status updated via CLI command
Evidence includes verification metadata for tool-verified
Proficiency and confidence are calculated correctly
verification: Test error handling for profile write failures

commit.message_template:
feat(learn): implement execute Phase 4 state update

Update KP status and profile with evidence-based scoring.

tasks.title: Implement Phase 5: Feedback and Next Steps in learn:execute
tasks.description: Implement feedback phase: display completion summary, suggest next steps, handle phase transitions.
implementation: Display completion summary: KP title, assessment result, time spent, proficiency gained
Calculate session progress: count completed KPs, total KPs, percentage complete
Determine next steps: find next pending KP in current phase, if current phase complete, suggest --next-phase, if all phases complete, congratulate and suggest learn:review
Display next KP suggestion: title, estimated time, prerequisites status
Offer actions via AskUserQuestion: 'Continue to next KP', 'Take a break', 'Review notes', 'Exit'
If user chooses 'Continue', recursively call learn:execute for next KP
If user chooses 'Review notes', display notes from sessions/{id}/interactions/notes/
Update session metadata: last_activity timestamp, total_time_spent
Display motivational message based on progress and profile.learning_preferences.focus

criteria: Completion summary includes all key metrics
verification: Test with KP in middle of phase
Test with last KP in phase
Test with last KP in session

commit.message_template:
feat(learn): implement execute Phase 5 feedback

tasks.title: Implement personalization logic across all phases
tasks.description: Integrate profile-driven personalization throughout the execution flow: resource presentation, learning order, time suggestions, difficulty adjustment.

modification_points.change: Add personalization logic based on profile data

implementation: Resource presentation (Phase 2): read profile.learning_preferences.style, filter and sort resources by style preference, adjust resource descriptions based on style
Learning order (Phase 1): read profile.learning_preferences.approach, if practice-first, suggest starting with code examples, if theory-first, suggest reading docs first

Time suggestions (Phase 2): read profile.time_availability.hours_per_week, calculate recommended session duration (15-60 min based on availability), display time estimate for each resource, warn if session exceeds recommended duration

Difficulty adjustment (Phase 2-3): read profile.experience_level and known_topics, if beginner, provide more detailed explanations and hints, if advanced, skip basic explanations, adjust assessment difficulty based on proficiency in related topics
Add personalization summary at start: display active profile, learning style, time availability, experience level

unit: Resources are filtered by style preference
Difficulty adjusts based on experience level

criteria: All 4 personalization dimensions are implemented (style, approach, time, difficulty)
Profile data is read and applied correctly

commit.message_template:
feat(learn): add comprehensive personalization

Profile-driven customization of resources, order, time, and difficulty.

tasks.title: Implement phase transition logic
tasks.description: Implement phase advancement logic: validate phase completion, update current_phase in state, update phase status in plan.
implementation: Add canAdvanceToNextPhase(plan, currentPhase) function: find all KPs in current phase, check if all are completed or skipped, return boolean

Add advancePhase(sessionId, currentPhase) function: validate canAdvanceToNextPhase, find next phase number, update state.current_phase via ccw learn:update-state, update plan.phases[currentPhase].status = 'completed', update plan.phases[nextPhase].status = 'active', display phase transition message
Integrate into --next-phase flag handling (Phase 1): call canAdvanceToNextPhase, if true call advancePhase, if false show error with incomplete KPs
Add phase status display: show current phase, completed phases, locked phases
Handle edge cases: last phase completion (no next phase), skipped KPs (count as complete for phase transition)

unit: canAdvanceToNextPhase returns true when all KPs complete
canAdvanceToNextPhase returns false when KPs pending
advancePhase updates state and plan correctly

criteria: State and plan are updated atomically
Edge cases are handled (last phase, skipped KPs)
Phase status is visible to user
verification: Test phase transition with all KPs complete
Test phase transition with pending KPs (should block)

commit.message_template:
